_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of system administration concepts for Unix/Linux and/or Windows operating systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.519), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.504).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.649). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, linux, operating, system]. DCWF-only terms [administration, concepts, systems, unix, windows] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.885 (P=0.898, R=0.873). P exceeds R by 0.025 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 32 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of system administration concepts for Unix/Linux and/or Windows operat" and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.695 ≈ STS 0.649 (gap 0.046). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge, linux, operating, system] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.6%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, linux, operating, system]. DCWF-only: [administration, concepts, systems, unix, windows]. WIDAI-only: [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals].
